title: aita for honking my horn at someone blocking the drive-thru?
text: this happened a while ago and is pretty small and petty, but i just stumbled back on this sub now and remembered that i wanted to ask it here when this happened. so i was in line for the drive thru one morning, and the car ahead of me looked like he was told the whole "it'll be a few minutes, pull up and we will bring it out to you" thing. so, he did, but i noticed he only barely pulled around the corner and the back half of the car was blocking the exit to the drive thru. he didn't move the whole time i waited for and got my order. so, as i pulled forward, i honked my horn because, i couldn't leave where he was sitting. i didnt lay on it or anything either, just a single beep. because our windows were down, i heard him swearing up a storm at me as he pulled forward. so, iata because i was too quick to honk and didn't wait to see if he would move when i got to his car, or was he just in a bad mood and what i did was fine?

storyboard.content: I was in line for the drive-thru one morning. The car ahead of me was told to pull forward and wait for his order.
The car ahead only barely pulled around the corner, and the back half of his car was blocking the exit to the drive-thru. He didn't move the entire time I waited for and received my order.
As I pulled forward, I honked my horn -- just a single beep -- because I couldn't leave where he was sitting.
Because our windows were down, I heard him swearing up a storm at me as he pulled forward.
I wonder if I was too quick to honk and should have waited to see if he would move when I got to his car.
